format_notes: Each item formatted to match bot input form. Categories follow PF2e conventions (Adventuring Gear, Alchemical, Material, Treasure, etc). Page numbers sequential for catalog organization.

items.description: A pulsing chunk of flesh harvested from an aberration, slick with otherworldly humors that defy normal anatomy. Used as a primary reagent when crafting mutagens or unstable potions, it grants a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks for such items. Spoils within 7 days unless preserved with alchemical salts.

An eye that sees what should not be seen, removed intact from an aberration's skull. When dissolved in a true seeing potion or used in a divination scroll, it doubles the duration of the effect. Brief contact with the eye causes the holder to glimpse fleeting visions of impossible geometries.

A severed aberrant tentacle, cured and made supple with curing oils. Crafted into a whip, it grants the finesse trait if it lacked one, and the weapon deals an additional 1 bludgeoning damage on a successful Trip attempt.

A vial of dark, viscous fluid that whispers when shaken. Used as the base for Confusion Oil and similar mind-affecting alchemical items, it imparts a +1 item bonus to the DC of mental effects in items it is used to craft.

Cerebral tissue still twitching in your hand, salvaged from a creature whose mind worked along impossible axes. A required component when crafting psychic-themed scrolls, wands, or talismans, reducing the material cost by 25%.
Hardened plates of aberrant shell, ridged and pitted in unfamiliar ways. When integrated into hide armor during crafting, the wearer gains a +1 item bonus to AC against aberrations.

A shimmering, faintly luminescent dust scraped from the cooling brain of a psionic creature. Used in mental resilience elixirs, it grants the resulting potion a +1 item bonus to saves against mental effects for 10 minutes.

Bone fused at unsettling angles, with subtly wrong joint structures. A weapon haft made from warped bone grants its wielder a +1 status bonus to Intimidation checks made to Demoralize while wielding it.
Translucent flesh that shifts between solid and ethereal at the edge of perception. A required reagent for crafting Blink and Dimension Door scrolls, halving the time required to scribe them.
A jar of clinging, faintly luminescent slime. Functions as a tanglefoot bag (lesser) when thrown, applying the immobilized condition until escaped (DC 17). Has a 5-foot splash.
Spinal fluid drawn from a mind-controlling aberration, shimmering with stolen thoughts. A required ingredient when crafting potions of suggestion or charm, increasing the spell's DC by 1.
The pebbled, shifting hide of an aberration, tanned with care to preserve its strange properties. Armor crafted from aberrant hide grants the wearer resistance 2 to mental damage.

A non-functional appendage cut from an aberrant body — extra limb, false eye stalk, or quivering protuberance. Scholars who study the Dark Tapestry will pay handsomely for an intact specimen, often double its base value.
A jagged crystal grown from the petrified terror of an aberration's victim. Used in confusion or fear-based magic items, it grants those items a +1 item bonus to their DCs.

A sliver of frozen void found embedded in the descendants of Great Old Ones. Required as a focus for planar travel rituals targeting the Dark Tapestry, the Far Realms, or other distant cosmic locations.
A peeled section of aberrant mucous membrane, sticky and chemically unstable. Used in adhesive bombs, it grants the bomb the sticky trait, persisting for 1 round after a hit.
A faintly twitching lump of unidentifiable aberrant tissue that may still be partially alive. Useful as a familiar component or as feed for breeding alchemical pests; spoils in 3 days unpreserved.
The hardened beak of a bird, raptor, or other beaked creature. Carved into a piercing tool, awl, or pen nib; ground beak makes a durable black ink valued by scribes.

A pungent gland extracted from a beast of similar species. Refined into perfume base, animal lure, or hunter's attractant — when used as bait, it grants a +2 item bonus to Survival checks to attract animals of the source's type.

A glandular sac harvested from beavers, capybaras, and similar rodents. Provides analgesic compounds used in mild pain relievers and a sweet-musk perfume base; ingestion grants a +1 item bonus to flat checks against mild pain effects.
Bitter green bile drained from a bear's gallbladder. A potent component for minor healing brews; an elixir crafted with bear bile heals an additional 1d4 HP on consumption.
The hooked, hollow stinger of a giant scorpion. Mounted as a dagger or arrowhead, it can hold a single dose of injury poison and grants a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to apply that poison.

A swirling silver vapor sealed in crystal, harvested from a creature native to the Astral Plane. Required as a focus when scribing scrolls of Astral Projection, halving the gp cost of the scroll's materials.
A length of severed silver cord, gleaming impossibly bright. Used in soul-binding magic, or woven into a magical rope that grants its wielder a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.

A crystal of solidified astral matter that hovers slightly above any surface it rests on. Functions as a substitute material for crafting low-grade aeon stones, storing 1 cantrip of stored spell energy when properly attuned.
A pinch of glittering dust scraped from the seams between planes. Tossed in the air, it functions as Dust of Disappearance for 1 round, granting greater invisibility in a 5-foot radius.

Glowing silver-blue blood from an astral creature, sealed in a crystal vial. When activated, it sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius for 1 hour and counts as sunlight for the purposes of damaging vulnerable creatures.
A jagged sliver of cold black nothing that drinks light from the air around it. Used to craft negative-energy weapons, granting an additional 1d6 negative damage on critical hits.

Hide from an astral creature, lighter than expected and faintly translucent at the edges. Armor crafted from plane-touched hide reduces its Bulk by 1 (minimum L) and grants resistance 3 to force damage.
Glittering powder collected from astral creatures, resembling pulverized stars. Sprinkled onto a divination focus, it grants a +2 item bonus to your next divination check made within 1 minute.

Tendons that gleam like spun moonlight. A bowstring made from starlight sinew allows the bow to fire arrows of pure light when no ammunition is loaded, dealing 1d6 damage with no range increment penalty within 60 feet.

A spectral organ removed from an astral creature, beating in time with a chosen location. Once tethered to a place, location, or person via a 1-hour ritual, it always points unerringly toward that target across any plane.

The still-warm heart of a magical beast, drawn out whole. Required to brew a Potion of Bestial Form or Animal Aspect, granting the resulting potion a +1 item bonus to a chosen attribute while transformed.

A tooth pried from a magical beast, gleaming with residual enchantment. A dagger crafted from a mystical fang counts as +1 striking and is treated as a magical weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistances.
A claw with traces of magical aura still clinging to it. Used as a focus when etching runes on handwraps of mighty blows, reducing the cost of inscribing potency runes by 10%.

Blood that retains a faint magical resonance, drawn from a beast within an hour of death. Refined into magical inks for scrolls or used as a healing reagent that adds +1 to the HP healed by any potion it is brewed into.
Rich, oily marrow from a magical beast's bones. A required component for strength-based elixirs; ingestion grants a +1 item bonus to Athletics for 10 minutes.
Sinew from a beast that channeled lightning, tingling faintly to the touch. A bowstring made from charged sinew adds 1d4 electricity damage to ranged attacks made with the bow.
The thick, feathered pelt of an owlbear, fierce and warm. Worn as a cloak, it grants cold resistance 2 and a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks against animals.
A heavy iron-gray scale from a gorgon, immune to its own breath weapon. Armor or shields incorporating gorgon scales grant the wearer a +1 item bonus to saves against petrification.

A bold, striped feather from a hippogriff's wing. Used as fletching grants the arrow the feather fall property if the wielder falls within 1 round of firing; also a required component for Feather Fall scrolls.

The fused triple heart of a chimera, each chamber distinct. Required ingredient when brewing a multi-attack mutagen; the resulting mutagen grants its imbiber an additional Strike on a successful Strike for 1 round, 1/day.
A massive, hooked talon from a griffon. Mounted onto a haft, it functions as a +0 climbing pick or as an aerial weapon (treat as light pick) with the agile trait.
A cloudy yellow eye that still seems to follow you across the room. Required component for crafting scrolls of Flesh to Stone; reduces the gp cost of the scroll by 25%.
A barbed, half-feathered half-scaled plume from a cockatrice. Carved into a quill, it serves as the writing implement for petrification spell scrolls.
The hollow, blood-draining needle of a stirge, surprisingly clean once flushed. Used as a medical leeching instrument or as the working end of a phlebotomist's tool.
A massive, ridged plate of bulette shell, harder than steel. A shield crafted from bulette shell gains +2 to Hardness and increases its HP by 5.
The shifting black hide of a displacer beast, never quite where you look for it. Used as the primary material for crafting a Cloak of Displacement (Lesser), reducing the gp crafting cost by 30%.
A long, gleaming white feather from a pegasus's wing. A required component when crafting a pair of Wings of Flying, halving the brewing time.
The pulsing, regenerating heart of a hydra, still trying to mend itself. Required ingredient for a Regeneration Elixir, granting fast healing 5 for 1 minute upon consumption.
A gleaming feather shed by an angel, faintly humming with celestial harmony. A required component when creating Holy Water, doubling the volume produced from a single ritual.

A shard of solidified light, broken from the halo of a slain celestial. Sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and required for crafting Daylight scrolls; the resulting scroll's daylight counts as natural sunlight.
Liquid gold drawn from a celestial's wound, warm and faintly luminous. Used in healing potion crafting, it grants +1 to the HP recovered when used in the Heal action.
A single tear shed by a celestial, captured before it could vanish. Used as the focal component for Lesser Restoration scrolls and rituals; halves the time to brew.
A bone from a celestial, white as polished moonstone and warm to the touch. Carved into a holy symbol that grants its wielder a +1 item bonus to attack rolls against creatures with the evil trait.
Glowing golden powder collected from a solar's wake. Sheds bright light in a 60-foot radius for 1 hour and counts as sunlight, dealing 1d8 damage to undead and fiends in the area each round.
Blood from a celestial, still warm and golden. The primary ingredient for Holy Water; one vial yields 4 doses of holy water when properly consecrated.

A radiant scale from a celestial dragon or drake, gleaming like polished electrum. Armor crafted with gold-touched scales gains the holy weapon property at no rune slot cost (must be applied during crafting).

The still-glowing heart of a celestial. A required ritual component for Resurrect; reduces the gp cost of the resurrection ritual by 50% and grants the caster a +2 circumstance bonus to the primary check.
Soft, white feathers shed naturally from a celestial. Used to trim sacred vestments; clothing trimmed in angelic plumage grants its wearer a +1 status bonus to Religion checks.
A vial of distilled celestial light, shimmering with divine grace. Used in crafting Moderate Healing Potions; the resulting potion heals 1d6 additional HP.
An eye still bright with divine sight, taken from an empyreal celestial. Required component for True Seeing scrolls; halves the time required to scribe.

The vocal apparatus of a Lillend, preserved with sacred salts. When integrated into a bardic instrument during crafting, the instrument grants its bearer a +1 item bonus to Performance checks and adds 1d4 sonic damage to performance-based spells.
A reed from the trumpet of a Trumpet Archon, eternally vibrating with divine song. Carved into a wind instrument, the player can cast Sound Burst (heightened to 2nd) once per day.
A fragment of a solar's flaming sword, still burning with divine fire. Required as the catalyst component when etching a Greater Flaming or Holy property rune; halves the rune's gp cost.

A pulsing crystal core that powered a construct, still humming with residual energy. Functions as a substitute spellheart with 1 cantrip charge, or can be used as a power source for crafting other constructs.
Spool of fine, faintly humming filament salvaged from a clockwork construct. A required material when crafting Clockwork Constructs or magical traps; grants +1 item bonus to the resulting trap's DC.
Heavy, articulated plates from a construct's body. When integrated into a shield during crafting, the shield gains +1 Hardness.
A bundle of small brass gears, springs, and pins. Required for crafting mechanical traps, clockwork tools, or timepieces; a clean set grants +1 item bonus to relevant Crafting checks.
A chunk of magic-imbued stone broken from a stone golem. Crafted into sling stones (10 per chunk) deals an additional 1 damage; or used to reinforce items, granting +1 to Hardness.

Hand-stitched panels of flesh harvested from a flesh golem. Required component for necromantic crafting and the assembly of new flesh constructs; can be applied as a healing patch granting fast healing 1 for 1 minute.

A pair of perfectly polished glass eyes from an animated mannequin or similar construct. Useful as decorative items or as a focus for scrying spells, granting a +1 item bonus to Scry's targeting check.

A crystalline heart that powered a golem, still pulsing with stored magic. Can hold a single spell of 4th rank or lower for later release; recharging requires a 1-day ritual and the matching spell slot.
A faceted gem with a glimmer of trapped soul-light still flickering inside. Required component for soul-binding rituals and the creation of bound undead servants.
An etched glyph carefully removed from a construct's chassis. Used as a focus for Animate Object scrolls, halving the gp cost to scribe.

An intact animating rune carved into a small plate. Affixed to a Tiny or smaller object during a 1-day ritual, it animates the object as a minor construct (using statistics for an Animated Tool) under the binder's command.
A handful of magically conditioned clay from a clay golem, malleable yet stable. Excellent for sculpting and used as a primary material in golem-construction or sympathetic magic.
A tightly coiled metal piece harvested from an inevitable or steam-driven construct. Integrated into armor, it grants the wearer fire resistance 2.

The still-warm heart of a dragon, twice the size of a man's torso. A potent ritual component for resistance potions and dragon-bound rituals; potions using a dragon heart grant +2 to the resistance value.
Tough, leathery membrane stretched between dragon wing-bones. Tents and cloaks crafted from dragon wing membrane grant resistance 5 to the dragon's energy type and weather no environmental damage.
The piercing, slit-pupiled eye of a dragon, preserved in oil. Required component for True Seeing scrolls (greater) or Crystal Ball construction; halves the gp crafting cost of the latter.
Cord-thick sinew from a dragon, strong enough to suspend a wagon. A bowstring made from dragon sinew grants the bow a +1 item bonus to damage rolls.
A spiraling, polished horn taken from a dragon's brow. Carved into a horn instrument that emits a fearsome blast — once per day, the wielder can cast Fear (1st rank) at DC 25 by sounding the horn.
The intact breath gland of a fire-breathing dragon, still warm to the touch. Required component for crafting a Greater Flaming property rune or 4 doses of Alchemist's Fire (Greater).
The frozen breath gland of an ice-breathing dragon, painfully cold to handle. Required component for crafting a Greater Frost property rune or 4 doses of Frost Vial (Greater).
An acid-leaking breath gland from a green or black dragon, kept in a glass jar. Required component for crafting a Greater Corrosive property rune or 4 doses of Acid Flask (Greater).
A breath gland that arcs with stray sparks. Required component for crafting a Greater Shock property rune or 4 doses of Bottled Lightning (Greater).

A black, ichor-streaked gland that fumes faintly when opened. Required component for a Greater Corrosive rune or yields 4 doses of a powerful injury poison (DC 28 Fortitude, 2d6 poison damage on each stage).
The dense, dark liver of a dragon, large enough to fill a barrel. Required component for crafting Antitoxin or Antiplague (major); a single liver yields 6 doses.

A polished stone removed from a dragon's gizzard, swirled with veins of metal. While carried, the bearer gains a +1 item bonus to Society checks to appraise treasure and to find hidden coins or gems within 30 feet (1/day, 1 hour duration).

An enormous, leathery egg, warm to the touch and pulsing faintly within. With proper care (Wisdom DC 30 over 6 months), it may hatch into a wyrmling of the appropriate dragon variety. Black-market value is staggering.

Coins, polished gems, and small valuables found in the dragon's stomach or gizzard. The bearer of dragon-gizzard treasure gains a +1 status bonus to Diplomacy checks against creatures who covet treasure (1/day for 1 hour).
A wickedly sharp spine harvested from a dragon's tail. Mounted on a polearm haft, it functions as a +0 striking glaive; or as massive ammunition for siege weapons (deals +1d8 damage).

A long, sensitive whisker plucked from a dragon's snout. Required component for Detect Magic scrolls (greater) or sensory enhancement potions, granting +2 to Perception for 10 minutes when used in a brew.
A jagged shard of frozen, faintly glowing flame from a fire elemental. Required component for crafting Alchemist's Fire (Greater); also functions as ammunition for elemental-themed weapons.

The crystalline core of a cold elemental, eternally weeping frost. Used to craft a Frost Vial (Greater) — instead of the standard 2d6 cold, it deals 4d6 cold and applies the slowed 1 condition for 1 round on a hit.
A pinch of solid storm-vapor harvested from an air elemental, crackling between the fingers. Used to craft Bottled Lightning (Major) or as material for shock weapon enhancements.
A dense block of stone from an earth elemental's core, harder than any quarried granite. Used in shield rune crafting; a shield reinforced with a granite core gains +1 to Hardness.
A jagged stone laced with veins of slow-flowing lava. Thrown as a bomb, deals 3d6 fire damage with 1d6 splash damage and creates difficult terrain in a 5-foot radius for 1 minute.
A jar of howling wind, pressurized and faintly cool. Released, functions as a Gust of Wind scroll (1/use); also used as a primary component for air-themed magic items.

A vial that endlessly refills with seawater, taken from a water elemental. Provides 1 gallon of fresh water per day when uncorked, or can be poured out as a Hydraulic Push (1st rank) effect once per day.

A pouch of fine, magically-tinged sand from a sand elemental. Useful in glassmaking (yields +10% volume) or as a fine abrasive for polishing weapons (grants +1 item bonus to a Crafting check to repair).
A pure white crystal of magical salt from a brine elemental. Sprinkled in a 5-foot circle, prevents incorporeal undead from crossing it for 1 hour.
A bottled cloud of smoke from a smoke elemental. When thrown, creates a 10-foot cloud of dense smoke for 1 minute, granting concealment to creatures within.
Hot ash gathered from a magma elemental's wake. A potent flux for blacksmithing (grants +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to forge metal items) or used in glasswork.
Magically dense mud from a mud elemental, dries to stone-hardness within a minute. Used as rapid-construction mortar; a single jar bonds 30 cubic feet of stone in 1 minute.
A bottle of writhing black mist, draining warmth from anything it touches. Required component for major necromancy crafting; halves the gp cost of crafting an undead-themed item.
A jar of pure, dancing white-gold light. When used to brew a healing potion, the potion heals an additional 1d8 HP and removes the sickened condition.
Slick, vaguely opalescent goo that lingers after an incorporeal undead is destroyed. Required component for etching the Ghost Touch property rune; halves the rune's gp cost.
Vaporous mist captured from a creature native to the Ethereal Plane. Required component for crafting Ethereal Jaunt scrolls; halves the time required to scribe.
Powdered ectoplasm with a faintly luminescent shimmer. Sprinkled on the air, reveals invisible creatures within 30 feet for 1 round; or used in invisibility potions to add 1 round of duration.
A scrap of impossibly thin gauze from the boundary between Material and Ethereal. Required as the primary fabric for crafting a Cloak of Etherealness, halving the gp cost.
Translucent membrane that briefly phases out of reality when struck. Armor crafted from phasing membrane lets the wearer reduce the first damage they take each round by 5 (force damage only).
A spectral metal forged from condensed ectoplasm, gleaming with otherworldly light. Weapons made of ghost silver bypass the resistances of incorporeal creatures and deal full damage to them.
A fragment of the wall between worlds, hard yet shifting in the hand. Required component for Pass Through Earth scrolls or rituals to bypass solid barriers.
The wispy, tail-like extremity of an ethereal creature. Functions as a quill that can write on incorporeal mediums and on smoke, water, or air; messages persist for 10 minutes.
Glittering dust shaken from fey wings. Used to craft Sleep Arrows (DC 19 Will save or fall unconscious for 1 minute) or as a primary component for dream-themed potions.
An iridescent pixie wing, fragile as a moth's. Used in Feather Fall potions to extend duration to 10 minutes, or as the focal component for minor flight scrolls.
A still-fluttering heart from a slain fey, glowing softly. Required component for charm or dominate person rituals; grants the caster +1 to the primary check.
Glowing pink blood drawn from a faerie's wound. Bottled, functions as a Faerie Fire scroll once per day; or used as the primary reagent in light-based potions.

A long strand of fey hair — silver, gold, or impossibly green. Used in subtle enchantment crafting; clothing or jewelry incorporating mystical hair grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.
A panel of bark peeled from a dryad's tree-bonded form, lightweight yet stronger than steel. A serviceable substitute for Darkwood, granting -1 Bulk on items crafted from it.
Captured swamp-light from a destroyed will-o'-wisp. Used in crafting a Lantern of Revealing or in light-based traps that deal 2d6 force damage and trigger truesight in the area.

A red felt cap soaked in the blood of countless victims. While worn, the wielder's weapon counts as +1 striking, but the wielder gains the cursed trait — they cannot rest until they have drawn blood with the weapon at least once that day.
Soft, glittering fur from a fey shapeshifter. Sewn into a cloak, the wearer can reroll a single failed save against an illusion effect once per day.
A spiraled satyr's horn carved into a set of pipes. Once per day, the player can attempt to charm all listeners within 30 feet (DC 19 Will save) for 1 minute.
An iridescent fey eye, still bright with second sight. Required component for True Seeing scrolls or potions; the resulting potion's True Seeing also reveals concealed faerie creatures.
A shimmering essence harvested from a fey illusionist. Required component when crafting Disguise Self scrolls or any illusion-based magical items; halves the gp cost.

Wine fermented from fey-grown grapes, glowing pink in the bottle. A drinker experiences vivid hallucinations for 1 hour, but gains a +2 status bonus to Performance checks during that time. DC 20 Fortitude or take a -1 status penalty to Will saves while affected.

A spiraling silver horn from a unicorn, warm with residual magic. The horn detects poison within 30 feet at will; the bearer can also cast Heal (3rd rank) once per day by touching the wounded with the horn. Can be made into a Holy Water analog (greater) by grinding.
Long, silver hair from a unicorn's mane. Used as the core for crafting healing-themed wands; or as a primary reagent in healing potions, granting +2 HP healed per dose.

A set of tiny crafting tools (knife, needle, awl) that work themselves when not observed. While left near a project for 8 hours, they make minor repairs or finishings — counts as Aid for one Crafting check per day.
A glowing acorn from a dryad's bonded tree, still tied to nature. Planted in soil and tended for 1 month, it grows into a Tiny awakened tree — a potential plant familiar with the Speech ability.
A pair of iridescent sprite wings. Used as fletching for arrows; arrows so fletched ignore concealment from cover and gain +1 to attack rolls.
Sinew from a quickling, taut and almost too quick to handle. Required component when crafting Boots of Speed (or Lesser); reduces gp cost by 25%.
The vocal sac of a boggard, distended and faintly croaking. Brewed into a Voice-Altering Potion (mimic any voice for 10 minutes), or used as a thunder bomb component to add 1d4 sonic damage.
A tear shed by a nymph, captured before it could vanish. Required component for crafting a Major Healing Potion, granting it +5 HP healed and removing one disease or poison effect.
Demon or devil hide tanned with brimstone. Armor crafted from fiendish hide grants fire resistance 2 and a +1 item bonus to AC against attacks made by good-aligned creatures.
A jagged horn from a demon or devil, still warm. Carved into an unholy symbol, it grants the wielder a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks made to Demoralize.
A wickedly serrated demon tooth, still smelling of brimstone. Mounted as a dagger, it counts as a magical weapon and adds +1 evil damage to attacks against good-aligned creatures.
A burning ember from a fiend's body, eternally smoldering. Thrown as a bomb, deals 4d6 fire damage and 1d6 evil damage with 2d6 splash; ignites unattended objects in a 10-foot radius.

A torn fragment of a contract written in infernal blood. While held, the bearer gains a +1 item bonus to Society checks regarding legal matters; if used as a focus during a binding agreement, the agreement gains supernatural enforcement.
Black, smoking blood from a slain fiend. The primary ingredient in Unholy Water (analog to Holy Water but deals damage to good creatures); also used in bane mutagens against good targets.
A leathery, batlike imp wing. Used as a focus for fiendish familiar bonding, or as a minor flight charm — once per day, the wearer can fall feather-light for 1 round.
A massive black scale from a pit fiend, immune to flame. Required material for crafting Hellish Armor (Greater); the resulting armor grants fire immunity 1/day for 1 minute.

A tear shed by a succubus or incubus, dark with regret. Required component for Charm Person scrolls (greater); used in cursed love potions that grant the target the charmed condition for 1 day (DC 26 Will save).
The cloven hoof of a fiend, blackened and scorched. Mounted as a fitting for cursed boots (grants speed but inflicts a -1 to Reflex saves), or rendered as an ingredient in haste potions.
A glowing red fiend eye that watches even after harvest. Used to brew Darkvision Potion (greater, 60-foot darkvision for 8 hours) or as a scrying focus.
A wisp of trapped damned soul, screaming faintly. Used as a focus in soul-binding rituals or in the inscription of unholy weapons; weapons so blessed deal +1d6 evil damage on a critical hit.
A barbed tail-tip from a fiend, pre-coated with infernal venom. Functions as a +1 dart that delivers a single dose of poison (DC 22 Fort, 1d6 poison damage and sickened 1).
A forked tongue from a devil or serpent-like fiend. Required component for a Glibness Potion (1-hour duration); the imbiber gains a +1 item bonus to Deception checks while the potion lasts.
Caustic black blood from an abyssal demon. Used to craft Acid Flask (Greater) or as a primary reagent in demonic enhancement mutagens (+1 status to Athletics for 1 minute, sickened 1 afterward).

An eye plucked from a hag's coven, still able to peer into other minds. Functions as a focus for a coven's communal scrying — three eyes used together can scry on any creature within 1 mile (DC 27 Will save).
Bone from a slain devil, blackened by infernal fire. Weapons crafted from devil bone count as magical and grant a +1 item bonus to attack rolls against good-aligned creatures.
A burning, sulfurous heart from a fire-aspected fiend. Used in fire-immunity rituals or as a primary reagent in Major Alchemist's Fire — boosts the bomb's damage by an additional 2d6.

Acidic saliva from a babau demon, sealed in a leather flask. Coats a weapon for 1 minute, adding 1 acid damage per strike, or applied to armor as a corrosive grease that damages opponents who hit the wearer (1 acid damage per hit for 1 minute).
A jet-black feather from an erinyes's wing. Used as fletching for crossbow bolts (counts as +1 striking and bypasses cover concealment by 1 step), or as a focus in truth-pact ritual components.
A swollen sac of fungal spores. Thrown, releases a 5-foot cloud of spores for 1 round; creatures within DC 17 Fort or fall asleep (light sleep for 1 round).
A coil of fungal mycelium, surprisingly tough and slightly elastic. Functions as 30 feet of basic cordage; will bind to most surfaces if dampened.
A jar of glowing fungal spores. Activated, sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius for 1 hour. Functions as a sunrod analog but does not count as sunlight.
A dried myconid cap, faintly twitching. Brewed into a communication potion that establishes mind-link telepathy with willing creatures within 30 feet for 10 minutes.
A withered, purple-veined stalk from a violet fungus. Used to brew an Energy Drain Ointment that reduces a struck creature's HP maximum by 1d4 for 1 minute (DC 19 Fort negates).
A piece of dried shrieker fungus, sensitive to motion and light. Functions as a basic alarm (loudly screeches if disturbed); also used as a primary component for shrieker traps.
A bottle of acrid yellow fluid extracted from various fungi. Used as a powerful adhesive (10 minutes set time) or as a component in antitoxin brewing.
A vial of dormant mold spores. Used as a disease component in cursed traps (DC 17 Fort or contract Mold Lung, sickened 1 for 1d4 days).
A rare magical truffle harvested by following sentient fungi. Highly prized by gourmets and alchemists; consumed, grants a +1 status bonus to a single skill check within 1 hour.
The pulsing fungal heart of a myceloid colony. Required component for Plant Growth or Awaken Plant scrolls; doubles the area affected by Plant Growth.
A bioluminescent fungus cap, dried and waxed. Sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius for 24 hours when activated by squeezing.
A single humanoid tooth, ground or whole. Used in folk magic and ritual reagents; may be valuable as a sympathetic component for divinations targeting the original owner.
A vial of fresh humanoid blood. Used as a sympathetic magic focus or as an ingredient in some ritual sacrifices; spoils within 24 hours unpreserved.
A still-warm humanoid heart, harvested with care. Required component for many necromancy rituals or as the focus in a lich phylactery's bonding ritual.
A preserved humanoid eye. Used as a scrying component (grants +1 item bonus to scry the eye's original owner) or in eye-of-the-beholder spell crafting.
A preserved humanoid tongue. Used as a Speak with Dead component (the dead must be the tongue's owner) or in voice-stealing potions that mimic the original owner's voice.
A heavy orc tusk, often carved with clan symbols. Functions as a bounty token in lawful regions (collected for 1-5 gp per tusk depending on region).
A pointed goblin ear, dried and strung. Functions as a bounty token (1 sp per ear typical); a string of goblin ears grants a +1 status bonus to Intimidation checks against goblins for 1 day.
A small, lightweight scale from a kobold. Used in crafting lightweight scale armor, or as a cheap dragon-scale substitute for decorative purposes.
The spotted, sun-dried pelt of a gnoll. Worn as a cloak in desert environments, grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks in arid biomes.
Tough, ridged scale from a lizardfolk warrior. Reinforces existing scale mail to gain +1 to Hardness; or used to craft new lightweight scale armor.
The musk gland of a troglodyte, retaining its noxious stench. Used to craft a Stinkbomb (DC 19 Fort or sickened 1 in a 10-foot radius for 1 minute) or to enhance other bombs by +1 to their save DC.
Thick, plush bugbear fur, surprisingly soft. Used to craft Quiet Boots (granting a +1 item bonus to Stealth) or padding for armor.
A square, blackened hobgoblin molar. Functions as a military bounty token in regions opposing hobgoblin armies; valuable to mercenary captains and recruiters.
The massive, still-warm heart of a giant. Required component for Bull's Strength potions; the resulting potion grants +2 status bonus to Athletics for 10 minutes.
Massive bones from a giant, useful for fortification or weaponry. A greatclub crafted from a giant's femur counts as a +0 striking weapon; or used as construction material.
A tooth from a hag in a coven, still bound by the coven's magic. Used as a focus in dark magic rituals; three teeth from the same coven function as a coven scrying focus.
A finely-made hand crossbow bolt, pre-coated with drow sleep poison. On a hit, target makes DC 22 Fort save or falls unconscious for 1 minute (drow sleep poison).
Wire-tough hair shaved from a duergar's beard. Braided into wire-rope or used to reinforce metal armor (granting +1 to Hardness when integrated).
Iridescent scale from a merfolk's tail. Integrated into armor, grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to Athletics (Swim) checks; or used in water-breathing potion crafting.
A long, sensitive whisker plucked from a ratfolk. Used as an ingredient in detect-trap potions; the brewer gains +1 item bonus to Perception checks for 1 hour to find traps.
A large, well-muscled centaur hoof. Crafted into light barding for horses, granting +1 item bonus to AC; or used in horseshoe crafting for swift mounts.

A dried halfling foot worn on a leather thong. Once per day, the bearer can reroll a single failed luck-related check (such as a flat check); however, the original halfling's family will hunt the bearer until the foot is returned (cursed trait).
A perfectly machined gear from an inevitable, geometrically pure. Used in clockwork construct crafting or as a primary lawful bane component (weapon deals +1d6 damage to chaotic creatures).
A black, gauzy veil from a psychopomp. Required component for soul-binding rituals or for Speak with Dead scrolls; halves the gp cost of the latter.
A black, twisted talon from a sahkil, exuding dread. Mounted on a weapon, the weapon gains the fear-causing property — on a critical hit, target makes DC 25 Will or becomes frightened 1.
A vial of impossibly shifting matter from a protean. Used as a primary component in polymorph spells (halves time to scribe scrolls); functions as a chaotic bane component for weapons.
A perfectly geometric crystal of pure law. Used in anti-chaos magic crafting; weapons crafted with axiomatic crystals deal +1d6 damage to chaotic creatures.
A smooth white stone from a monitor of truth. Once per day, the bearer can activate it to detect lies for 1 hour (DC 23 Will save to deceive).
A small shard of crystallized law. Used as a primary reagent for lawful weapon enhancements; required for Banishment scrolls.
Glittering dust from chaos-aligned monitors. Used to craft a Confusion Bomb that on a hit causes the target to make DC 22 Will or become confused for 1 round; 1d6 mental splash.

A black coin recovered from a psychopomp's pouch. When placed in the mouth of a dying creature, it grants +2 status bonus to recovery checks against the dying condition; can also pay Pharasma's ferryman during a Resurrection ritual to reduce gp cost by 25%.
A jar of viscous, color-changing ooze slime. Functions as the base for tanglefoot bag crafting; or used as a versatile adhesive.
A cooling, soothing gel from a gentle ooze. Functions as a minor healing salve (heals 1d4 HP when applied as a 2-action activity).
A flask of intensely acidic ooze bile. Used to craft Acid Flask (Greater) — yields 2 doses per flask of bile — or as a corrosive coating for weapons (1 acid damage per strike for 1 minute).
A pouch of unusually sticky mucus from a clinging ooze. Used as a heavy-duty glue (sets in 1 round, holds 50 lbs.) or as a primary trap component.
A wobbling fragment of gelatinous cube. Coats a rope, granting climbers a +1 item bonus to Athletics (Climb) for 1 hour; or thrown to slow targets in a 5-foot square.
Yellowish residue scraped from an ochre jelly. Coats a weapon as a paralytic injury poison (DC 19 Fort or paralyzed for 1 round on hit); 1 dose per residue.
A jar of writhing black pudding ooze. When applied to a non-magical metal or wooden item, the item takes 2d6 acid damage per round until consumed (or until cleaned, 1 action).
Preserved slime mold, dried into a hard cake. Used as a coating to prevent further mold contamination on stored goods (lasts 1 month) or as the base for survival rations.
The severed tip of an ooze's pseudopod, jiggling lightly. A curio for alchemists and naturalists; valuable as a teaching specimen.
A pulsating, heart-like nucleus from an unusually old ooze. Required component for crafting a Regeneration Ointment (fast healing 2 for 1 minute when applied).
Drifting pollen from a magical plant. Used to craft Sleep Arrows (DC 20 Will or unconscious) or as a primary component in charm-themed dust traps.
A leaf that whispers when nothing else moves. Required component for druidic spell scrolls (e.g., Speak with Plants, Plant Growth); halves the gp cost of these scrolls.

A panel of bark peeled from a treant, alive and breathing slowly. A serviceable Darkwood substitute (-1 Bulk on items crafted from it); also functions as a living staff that grants its bearer a +1 item bonus to Nature checks.

A 30-foot length of slowly writhing sentient vine. Functions as a Living Rope — once per day, can be commanded to entangle a target within 30 feet (DC 20 Reflex save); also a required component for Entangle scrolls.
A length of tough plant tendril. Used as a strong cordage substitute (50 feet per harvested length); or as a whip-craft material for druids who eschew leather.
A piece of heartwood cut from a magical tree's core, glowing softly. Used as a wand-quality wood; staves and wands made from heartwood gain +1 to spell attack rolls or DCs at the GM's discretion.
A petal from a magical or sentient flower. Used as the primary aromatic in charm potions; the resulting potion grants the imbiber +1 item bonus to Diplomacy for 10 minutes.
A fat tuber-like root from a magical plant. A common ingredient in strength tonics; the resulting tonic grants +1 status bonus to Athletics for 10 minutes.
A young vegepygmy sprout, freshly potted. With proper care over 1 week, can be bonded as a plant familiar; or used as a primary component for Speak with Plants (Lesser) scrolls.
A screaming, twisted humanoid-shaped root. Used to brew a Greater Sleep Potion (DC 24 Will or unconscious 1 hour) or as the principal reagent in fear bombs.

The dense, vine-wrapped core of a shambling mound, drinking ambient electricity. Required component for armor with electricity resistance — armor incorporating a shambling mound heart grants the wearer electricity resistance 5.
Bright yellow pollen from a yellow musk creeper. A potent charm-based dust (DC 24 Will or charmed for 1 minute) or as a primary reagent in mind control elixirs.
A branch from a quickwood tree, still living. Functions as a +0 club, and the wielder gains a +1 status bonus to Athletics checks while wielded.
A bottled wisp of spirit-essence. Required component for Speak with Dead scrolls or for Bind Spirit rituals; halves the gp cost of the latter.
A shard of trapped soul-stuff. Used in soul-binding rituals or as the focal component for crafting a phylactery analog (functions as a lich's phylactery for non-lich casters at GM's discretion).
A captured memory crystallized into a faint, faceted shard. Required component for Modify Memory scrolls or as a focus for Reincarnate rituals.

A glass vial containing a single bottled emotion (joy, grief, anger, etc.). Used as a bardic component, granting +1 item bonus to Performance checks invoking that emotion; or activated to project the emotion in a 10-foot aura for 1 round.
A panel of glass that briefly displays the faces of nearby spirits. Required component for Crystal Ball construction or as a stand-alone scrying focus, granting +1 item bonus to scrying attempts.
Silver-gray ash from a destroyed spirit. Used as the primary reagent for invisibility powder (1 dose grants invisibility for 1 round) or as a ghost form ointment.
Dust collected from an ancestral spirit's manifestation. Required for Ancestral Guidance scrolls; halves the gp cost. Also used in spirit-binding rituals.
A weeping spirit's tear, silver and shimmering. Used in Etheric Vision components; the bearer gains a +1 item bonus to Perception checks against incorporeal creatures for 1 day after consumption.
A spirit-token bound to a specific Loa. Functions as a focus for possession rituals or as a spirit-binding charm; once per day, the bearer can call upon the bound Loa for guidance (treat as Augury).
A captured echo of a spirit's voice. Required component for Message scrolls (greater range) or whispered command items.
A mote of pure entropy from a destructive spirit. Required ingredient for Disintegrate potions; the resulting potion deals 4d10 damage on consumption to a touched target (DC 25 Fort halves).

A skull preserved with embalming alchemy, eyes faintly glowing. Required component for Animate Dead spells or as a necromancy focus, granting +1 item bonus to necromancy spell DCs for 1 round when invoked.

Rotting flesh from an undead, kept malign with necromantic preservation. Used as a primary ingredient in disease-themed traps, or as the active component in Ghoul Touch ointment (paralyzes target for 1 round on hit, DC 20 Fort).
A pristine vampire fang, still razor-sharp. Required component for Charm Person scrolls or for crafting a vampiric weapon enhancement that grants the wielder 1d6 temporary HP on a critical hit.
A shard of a destroyed lich phylactery, still humming with corrupted magic. Required component for soul-binding rituals; use with extreme caution — improper handling may bind the user's soul instead.

Sticky ectoplasm from a corporeal undead manifestation. Required component for Ghost Touch property runes (halves rune gp cost) or for crafting minor weapons that strike incorporeal creatures normally.
Sanctified dust from old graves. Used as a base reagent for Animate Dead components, or sprinkled in a 5-foot circle to ward against undead for 1 hour (DC 17 Will to cross).
Linen wrappings from a mummy, still bearing dust of ages. When worn as bandages or armor padding, grants resistance 1 to all damage but the wearer takes a -1 status penalty to Reflex saves (cursed).
A blackened claw from a ghoul, still bearing paralytic toxin. Mounted as a dagger, the wielder can apply 1 dose of paralysis poison per day (DC 20 Fort or paralyzed for 1 round).

A vial of bottled wraith-essence, cold to the touch. Required component for energy drain weapon enhancements; weapon deals an additional 1d4 negative damage and drains 1 HP from target on a critical hit.
The desiccated, rotting heart of an undead. Used as a focus for necromantic rituals or as a Create Undead component, granting +1 item bonus to the resulting undead's HP.

A length of stark white hair from a banshee, still vibrating with sorrow. Required component for Wail of the Banshee scrolls; halves the gp cost. Also used as the active ingredient in sonic damage components.
A blackened plate from a death knight's armor, dense and runed with hate. Major armor crafting material; armor incorporating death knight plate grants the wearer resistance 2 to slashing damage.
A misty, half-tangible veil from a specter. Required component for crafting a Shadowmeld Cloak — once per day, the wearer can become invisible for 1 round in dim light or darker.
A glassy wight eye, still seeing in darkness. Used to brew a Greater Darkvision Potion (8 hours, 60-foot darkvision); also a component for life drain weapon enhancements.
A femur from a skeletal undead. Wielded as a +0 club; or used as a focus for low-level necromancy spells, granting +0 item bonus (useful for non-magical shrines).
A blackened, rotted tooth from a zombie. Used as a disease component in rot-arrow poisons (DC 16 Fort or sickened 1 for 1 hour on hit).
A skeletal finger from a lich, eternally cold. Used as the core of a Wand of Cold (Lesser); or as the principal component in Finger of Death scrolls.

The vestigial heart-place of a dread wraith, still ripples with negative energy. Used as the focus for crafting a Soul Cage; or as a primary ingredient in energy drain weapon enchantments (deals +1d6 negative damage on hit).
An eye from a bodak, still capable of inflicting death-gaze. Used to craft a death-gaze weapon (1/day, target makes DC 27 Fort or takes 6d6 negative damage) or in greater fear potions.
A pulsing crystal-cage from a devourer, capable of holding a soul. Functions as a soul-trapping focus for Trap the Soul rituals; or as the central component in major necromantic creation rituals.

Tattered black cloth from a vampire's cloak, still smelling of blood. Required material for crafting a Cloak of Bats; the resulting cloak grants its wearer the ability to transform into a swarm of bats once per day for 10 minutes.
The long, prehensile tongue of a mohrg. Used as a primary ingredient in paralysis weapon enchantments (target hit makes DC 24 Fort or paralyzed 1 round); also a component for Bestow Curse scrolls.
